rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM ROUSE VS. CHEVRON CORP. et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, April 6, 2010, line 2, DEFENDANT CHEVRON U.S.A. INC. DEMURRER TO THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff fails to allege that Chevron is responsible for his damages. Plaintiff does not identify the allegedly injury-producing toxin, exposure to sample manufactured or supplied by Chevron, or allege how the toxin entered his body. (See Bockrath vs. Aldrich Chemical Co. (1999) 21 Cal. 4th 71.) Fraud is not alleged with specificity. No facts establish extreme or outrageous conduct by Chevron to support emotional distress claim. No reasonable probability that defects can be cured by amendment. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM ROUSE VS. CHEVRON CORP. et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, April 6, 2010, line 2, DEFENDANT CHEVRON U.S.A. INC. MOTION TO STRIKE THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT. Motion granted without leave to amend. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: TRINITY MANAGEMENT SERVICES VS. SARAH KANTNER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Sarah Kantner'S Motion To Set Aside Default/Default Judgment (Transferred From Dept 301)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, April 6, 2010, line 13, Defendant Sarah Kantner's Motion to Set Aside Default/Default Judgment (Transferred From Dept 301). Motion is granted. The motion makes clear the grounds for the relief sought. (See Juri vs. Greenwald (2003) 107 Cal.App. 4th 1119, 1125.) Defendant's failure to appear at the ex parte hearing excusable, and judgment was entered without opposition due to her inadvertence, surprise and excusable neglect. =(302/CWW)
